content.problemStatement:
There are NNN players participating in a knockout tournament. The iii-th player has the rating AiA_iAi‚Äč. No two players have the same rating.

N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 matches are played in the tournament. In each match, 222 random players are chosen out of the remaining players, and the player with the higher rating wins. The losing player gets eliminated from the tournament.

Can you find out the expected rating of the player ending up at the second position (that is, the expected rating of the player losing in the (N‚ąí1)(N-1)(N‚ąí1)-th match)?

The expected rating of the second player can be represented as a fraction of the form PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äč. You are required to print P‚čÖQ‚ąí1(mod1‚ÄČ000‚ÄČ000‚ÄČ007)P \cdot Q^{-1} \pmod{1 \, 000 \, 000 \, 007}P‚čÖQ‚ąí1(mod1000000007).


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains NNN - the number of players participating in the tournament.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚čĮ‚ÄČ,ANA_1, A_2, \cdots , A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚čĮ,AN‚Äč - the ratings of the players.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line the expected rating of the second player modulo 1‚ÄČ000‚ÄČ000‚ÄČ0071 \, 000 \, 000 \, 0071000000007.
constraints: Ai‚Č†AjA_i \ne A_jAi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Aj‚Äč for all 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: There is only one possible match, and the player with rating =1= 1=1 will be the second player.


Test Case 222: The player with rating =1= 1=1 will be the second player with probability 13\frac{1}{3}31‚Äč. The player with rating =2= 2=2 will be the second player with probability 23\frac{2}{3}32‚Äč. Therefore, the expected rating of the second player is 1‚čÖ13+2‚čÖ23=531 \cdot \frac{1}{3} + 2 \cdot \frac{2}{3} = \frac{5}{3}1‚čÖ31‚Äč+2‚čÖ32‚Äč=35‚Äč.
